beats.title: Glory of Kashi and Departure of Divodasa
beats.summary: This chapter from the Skanda Purana, Kashi Khand (part 3), describes the glory of Kashi (Varanasi), the departure of King Divodasa, the entrance of Yoginis into Kashi, the eight types of marriage, the practice of Brahma-yajna and other good conduct, the story of Kalaraja and Kalabhairava, the greatness of the Ganges, and the enumeration of duties for different ashramas.

beats.title: The Glory of Kashi and the Story of Harishchandra
beats.summary: The chapter describes the glory of Kashi, the importance of the Manikarnika Ghat, and the story of King Harishchandra who attained liberation through truthfulness. It also narrates the tale of a Brahmin who achieved moksha by bathing in the Ganga at Kashi.

beats.title: The Glory of Kashi and Manikarnika
beats.summary: The chapter describes the glory of Kashi (Varanasi) and the importance of the Manikarnika Ghat. It narrates the story of how Lord Vishnu's discus (Sudarshana Chakra) was used to create the sacred tank Manikarnika, and how various deities and sages extol the virtues of Kashi. The chapter emphasizes that dying in Kashi grants liberation (moksha) and that the city is the most sacred pilgrimage site.
